title: Depth, Subgrid, Warp, Particles & Spring FAB
description: Five fresh UI patterns: scroll-driven 3D depth reveal, CSS subgrid magazine, SVG turbulence warp, spring particle burst, and a radial FAB menu.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Depth Reveal
drops.description: Cards tilt in from unique 3D angles as they enter the viewport — pure CSS, no JS.
drops.concept: Uses animation-timeline: view() with animation-range: entry 0% entry 40% so each card completes its reveal in the first 40% of its scroll entrance. A CSS custom property --rot-y gives every card a unique initial Y-rotation angle, and filter: blur() syncs from 8px to 0 for a camera-lens focus effect. Zero JavaScript involved.
drops.source: CSS scroll-driven animations spec (W3C) + Chrome implementation

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to trigger each card's reveal

drops.title: CSS Subgrid Magazine
drops.description: 12-column subgrid layout with articles spanning variable widths, category filter, and click-to-expand.
drops.concept: A 12-column CSS grid with grid-template-rows: subgrid allows article children to inherit parent column tracks for perfect baseline alignment across asymmetric spans. Category filtering uses a class toggle on the parent container with :not() selectors — no JS state management needed. Articles click-expand to full-width using a class toggle.

interaction.mouse: Click articles to expand; use filter buttons to show categories

drops.title: SVG feTurbulence Text Warp
drops.description: Hover words to warp them with unique SVG displacement maps — tunable frequency, scale, and octaves.
drops.concept: Each word has its own SVG <filter> with feTurbulence (varying type, seed, and baseFrequency) piped into feDisplacementMap. On hover, a JS spring animation eases the displacement scale from 0 to the target value, while simultaneously animating the turbulence baseFrequency for organic undulation. Three sliders let the visitor tune global displacement scale, noise frequency, and octave count in real time.

interaction.mouse: Hover words to distort; click for burst; drag sliders to tune

drops.title: Spring Particle Burst
drops.description: Each of 5 buttons emits a unique species of physics particles — shapes, gravity, and shockwave rings.
drops.concept: On click, a burst of 30-45 Canvas 2D particles are emitted from the button center with randomized angles, speeds, and spring-oscillation dampening applied per-particle. Each button has a distinct palette, particle count, gravity constant, and drag coefficient. A shockwave ring expands from the origin using eased lerp. Particles use 5 shape types (circle, square, star, triangle, ring) drawn with ctx transforms.
drops.source: Canvas particle physics techniques (game dev community)

drops.title: Radial Spring FAB Menu
drops.description: Material-style FAB that expands 5 action items radially with staggered spring CSS transitions.
drops.concept: A Floating Action Button rotates 45° on open, while 5 child buttons spring outward to pre-calculated positions using CSS transition with cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) — the standard spring overshoot curve. Each item has an incrementally larger transition-delay for staggered entrance. A backdrop overlay with backdrop-filter:blur closes the menu on outside click. Labels appear on hover with a slide-in transition.
drops.source: Material Design 3 FAB specification + CSS spring easing patterns

interaction.mouse: Click + to open menu; hover items for labels; click outside to close
interaction.touch: Tap + to open; tap items or outside to close
interaction.keyboard: Space to toggle, Escape to close
